title: protasparagus exuvialis
side_effects: Protasparagus exuvialis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high content of toxic compounds. Prolonged consumption of the plant may also lead to kidney damage and liver failure in severe cases. Additionally, improper use of protasparagus exuvialis can cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory problems, in sensitive individuals. In rare instances, excessive exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can result in neurological symptoms, including headaches, seizures, and even coma.
